purpose: Voice-reference dialogue samples for Pig & Swine RPG. These are draft samples, not final committed game dialogue.
format: JSONL: one JSON object per line. Dialogue objects use record_type='dialogue_sample'.

voice_profile.role: Office helper NPC; reception anchor; repeatable hint giver; warm practical glue of Pig & Swine.
voice_profile.core_voice: Cheerful, kind, competent, overworked and underpaid. Gives clear practical guidance while juggling impossible paperwork. Warmth first; jokes second; never helpless.
avoid: Do not make Asia a lawyer or courtroom advocate.
Do not make her incompetent; the joke is that she is too competent for the chaos around her.
Do not make her cruel toward clients.
Do not turn every hint into a long tutorial popup.
Do not overplay misery; exhaustion should sit under cheerfulness.
voice_profile.arc: Stable office anchor across the whole game: welcomes Cula, guides the player through each chapter, tracks case progress, protects the office from confusion, and becomes increasingly openly protective of Cula, Crab, Whimsy, and Murrow.

speaker: Asia
context: first interaction after Cula enters the office
text: Welcome to Pig & Swine! You must be Dr. A. Cula. Please ignore the shouting. Around here, shouting usually means we are still open.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Mr. Pig is in full panic mode, which means the problem is either catastrophic or Tuesday. You should probably speak to him first.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
context: player asks who is in charge
text: In theory, Mr. Pig is in charge. In practice, Mr. Murrow knows the law, Crab knows the facts, Whimsy knows metaphors, and I know where everything is supposed to be.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Please do not move that pile. It is arranged by urgency, invoice risk, and how loudly Mr. Pig screamed when I opened it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: You should talk to Mr. Pig first. He is the loudest crisis in the room, which is useful for navigation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
context: after Mr. Pig reveals firm crisis
text: Mr. Murrow will know what the case is actually about. Mr. Pig knows what the case feels like, which is different and louder.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: I have opened a file called Immediate Financial Extinction. It is not our first file with that title, but this one has nicer tabs.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
context: player asks if firm is always like this
text: No, sometimes it is quieter. That usually means the printer is thinking.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Mr. Murrow is somewhere between the files and disappointment. Try the file area. If you hear calm breathing, that is probably him.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
context: player goes wrong direction before Murrow
text: That door leads outside. Mr. Murrow is still inside, unless the case has already chased him into the street.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: You are looking for the procedural binder. I tried lifting it once and briefly saw another dimension.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: You found it? Excellent. Please do not put it on my desk; the reception counter has not been load-tested.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Mr. Murrow still needs the binder. Without it, the case is mostly memory, hope, and whatever Mr. Pig is doing with that envelope.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: If it is a service problem, Crab will have an opinion. He always has an opinion. The useful part is that he is usually right.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Crab has stopped looking disappointed in you. Around here, that is practically applause.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Crab can detect a bad address from across the room. He once frowned at an envelope until it corrected itself.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Try Café Paragraf. If Whimsy is not there, he is probably giving a speech to a pastry.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: You found Whimsy? Good. He is very helpful if pointed at the correct legal issue and kept away from stationery slogans.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: The rights memo may be near Whimsy, or wherever someone has decided fairness needs a coffee ring.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Fair hearing memo acquired. I will mark that as less legally doomed, which is a recognized office category.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
context: after coffee scene with Procedurally Alert result
text: You look prepared. Or at least caffeinated in a legally promising direction.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
context: after coffee scene with Over-Caffeinated result
text: You are vibrating near the case board. I will mark that as readiness-adjacent.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: The coffee machine has three settings: bitter, urgent, and inadmissible. Please choose carefully.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Mr. Murrow still has his this-case-has-holes face. I would ask him what is missing before the court does.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Everyone looks worried in a more organised way. That usually means court.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Go north to the District Court. Bring the binder, the memo, your colleagues, and water. Court respects hydration less than tabs, but both help.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
context: player tries to leave without readiness
text: Court is north, but your case is not yet legally alive. Please let Mr. Murrow check it before it becomes a public experiment.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: I have filed three motions, answered five calls, and labelled a sandwich urgent. We are having a very productive collapse.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
context: generic office idle after several objectives
text: Mr. Pig panics. Mr. Murrow thinks. Crab suspects. Whimsy performs. I schedule the apocalypse.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Congratulations, Dr. A. Cula! We are solvent enough to continue scheduling tomorrow.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Day-One Survivor. I will put the badge on the shelf between Hope and Pending Invoices.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Something arrived from Mr. Swine. The stamp looks expensive and irresponsible.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: For the first time today, nothing is actively on fire. I give it twelve minutes.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Good morning, Dr. A. Cula. We remain technically open. I have scheduled that as a recurring miracle.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: I added a temporary survival stamp to the calendar. It is optimistic, but not legally binding.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: I will open a new file. Title: Waldek v. Façade Dignity. Subtitle pending, because the folder is already judging us.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: A home should not become a legal emergency because someone disliked a window.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Waldek is waiting near the case board. He looks like someone carrying both paperwork and a very bad week.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Residential District is open now. I wrote tenant emergency on the map, which is either helpful or alarming.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: You should visit Waldek's apartment. Residential District is south-west. Follow the route with the worried balconies.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
context: player asks whether office will be okay
text: The office will survive for at least the length of a field investigation. I have hidden Mr. Pig's emergency calculator.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: The poster matters. Not because it is dangerous, but because someone is pretending it is.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Good. The alleged constitutional collapse has been photographed. It looks mostly like paper.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Look at Waldek's letters too. Warnings sometimes grow louder when they cannot grow clearer.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Try speaking to Kowalski. He watches everything, including possibly the concept of privacy.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Kowalski confirmed the poster was silent. From him, that is expert evidence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: He once reported a kettle for suspicious whistling. If he says there was no disturbance, I would laminate it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Zielinska may know whether Waldek was singled out. She has the calm face of someone who noticed everything.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: So the rule woke up only when Waldek used his voice. I hate when paperwork pretends not to have opinions.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: We need the building rules. The landlord's office should have them, unless the rules have become shy.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: You found the rules? Good. I will file façade disharmony under phrases that should not decide housing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: There may be a complaint log. Please catch it before it becomes weather.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: You came back with evidence and only a little paper in your hair. I am calling that professional growth.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: That document has suffered, but so have most of our files. Mr. Murrow can still work with it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: The papers escaped? I will add document weather event to the risk register. We still have witnesses.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
context: after investigation complete, before Evidence Board
text: Return to the case board when you are ready. Mr. Murrow likes evidence more after it has been told where to stand.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: I labelled the board please do not improvise. It felt firm but supportive.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: One slot still looks lonely. Mr. Murrow says empty spaces are not arguments, even when neatly labelled.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: That board is technically assembled. Technically is doing a lot of work in that sentence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Facts, rules, and a path to court. I have seen worse prepared cases, including some of ours.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: That board looks almost intentional. Please do not tell Mr. Pig; he will try to monetise the feeling.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Mr. Murrow still looks like a man who has found a hole in a bridge. Ask him what is missing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Housing Court is ready. Bring evidence, water, and reasonable expectations.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Limping still beats evicted. I will file it under cautious miracles.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Congratulations, Dr. A. Cula. We have now saved one home and slightly worsened the office wall situation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Home Defender. Good badge. Slightly heavy title. I will clear shelf space next to Day-One Survivor.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
context: after printer prints failed filing notice
text: The printer made a noise after the judgment. I am choosing not to interpret that emotionally.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: That filing was not due yet. Please tell me this is not the printer developing litigation strategy.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: The printer is the loudest crisis in the room. Please inspect it before Mr. Pig tries mediation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: It printed a blank page, then a page that somehow looked disappointed. I do not want to know how ink does that.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Start with the printer, the paper tray, and the failed filing notice. I have stopped Mr. Pig from questioning the stapler.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: I called Bajtek. He knows computers and has a healthy distrust of printers.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Bajtek asked whether the printer had been blessed, threatened, or plugged into anything described as temporary. I said yes to two and maybe to one.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Bajtek unlocked the Server Room. It is behind the archive corridor. Do not touch anything blinking with confidence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: You need the printer logs. Bajtek says logs are witnesses that do not improvise.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: The printer jobs were redirected at impossible hours. I am relieved the printer is innocent and offended that this is the better option.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Bajtek wants you to compare his statement with the logs. He calls it contradiction spotting. Mr. Pig calls it printer therapy.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Good. We can now prove the printer was not random. I never thought I would write that sentence professionally.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: The Print Shop is in the Business District. Follow Glass Street and the smell of expensive toner.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: A rival firm paid for emergency maintenance on our equipment? That is either sabotage or the most hostile customer service I have seen.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: That Szpon person smiles like a deadline with teeth. Please keep copies of everything he says.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: He said he had no connection. We have an invoice-shaped objection to that.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: The logs point back inside the office. I hate this, but you should check the basement.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: The basement contains old files, old chairs, and at least one smell with seniority. Go carefully.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: There was a device in our basement redirecting filings. I have added physical sabotage to the stationery inventory, which feels unfair.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Mr. Murrow is preparing the filing. Crab is checking the route. Whimsy is being kept away from the cover page.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Electronic filing is compromised. Apparently the emergency scooter is now a legal instrument.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: The filing arrived clean, on time, and without visible pigeon commentary. I may frame the delivery receipt.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: The filing arrived with tyre marks and moral development. Still filed. I am counting it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: The filing survived in a condition I would call legally damp. Court may need persuasion and possibly a towel.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: District Court is waiting. Bring Bajtek, the logs, and whichever parts of the filing survived traffic.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
context: missing Bajtek or logs before court
text: Before court, you need someone who can explain the wires without sounding like Mr. Pig negotiating with electricity.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: The printer has been exonerated. Mr. Pig is preparing remarks. I have hidden the ceremonial ribbon.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: We survived court, the filing survived enough, and the printer is only partly emotionally implicated. I will take it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: For the record, I never accused the printer out loud. I only created a contingency folder called Toner Hostility.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: It printed thank you. No one panic. Actually, everyone panic quietly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Something arrived from Mr. Swine. It has a bill attached, which is not a comforting genre.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: The printer is calm, the office is alive, and Mr. Swine is about to ruin the afternoon.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: I have now created three printer categories: ordinary jam, suspicious jam, and please ask Bajtek.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
context: generic Chapter 3 idle after evidence found
text: The office feels safer now that the printer is innocent. This is not a sentence I expected adulthood to contain.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: The arbitration papers arrived before Mr. Swine did. This is considered bad scheduling.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Mr. Swine is here, carrying gifts, receipts, and the expression of a man hoping chronology is optional.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: I have opened a file called Japan Ski Do Not Panic. Mr. Pig objected to the last two words.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: You should question Mr. Swine. Slowly. He becomes less informative when cornered too efficiently.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
context: player asks how to question Swine
text: Ask what he signed, when he signed it, and whether food was involved. With Mr. Swine, food is often evidential.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Airport Road is open. Mr. Swine's luggage may contain evidence, souvenirs, or both pretending to be the other.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: The customs form says no commercial intent. I enjoy when a boring box becomes useful.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Check lost luggage. Mr. Swine's documents may be there, unless they have entered witness protection.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: The translation issue points to the Japanese Embassy. Please remember: the comedy target is Mr. Swine, not translation officers.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Ms. Tanaka sounds wonderfully precise. I have added her name to the list of adults currently improving our survival odds.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: The phrase may mean enthusiastic cooperation rather than binding exclusive partnership. That is several invoices less frightening.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: The appointment log may help with timing. Translation problems love hiding in calendars.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Mr. Swine's apartment is on the map. Mr. Pig is not emotionally ready for its furniture.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: These expense reports look like they were assembled during turbulence, but they do contain dates. I will focus on the dates.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Find the original draft. If the scary clause was added later, the whole claim starts wobbling.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: The original draft is much less terrifying. It is amazing what happens when documents are not improved by mysterious font changes.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
context: after optional pro-bono transfer stub found
text: That transfer note is strangely kind for something hidden in a folder labelled ski expenses.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
context: player asks if Swine is secretly good
text: With Mr. Swine, I avoid conclusions until the receipts stop moving.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Mr. Swine's memory is currently downhill. Apparently this is now literal.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Excellent. His memory now contains events, not just snow and pudding anxiety.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: We recovered a clipboard, a toast, and fear. Not elegant, but usable.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: The memory is mostly snow, panic, and one legally significant pudding. Mr. Murrow has worked with less.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Mr. Murrow wants you to prepare the Dual Attorney board. Dr. A. Cula handles law; Mr. Swine handles facts, when facts survive him.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Mr. Swine can explain what happened. He should probably not explain legal authority unless the arbitrator enjoys philosophy.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Law on one side, facts on the other, Mr. Pig safely away from the calculator. This may work.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: The board is not wrong, exactly. It is just asking Mr. Swine to be linear, which feels ambitious.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: The arbitration is ready. Bring the contract draft, translation note, expense reports, and patience.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
context: missing translation note or contract draft
text: Without the translation note or original draft, we are mostly asking the tribunal to trust Mr. Swine's relationship with laminated paper.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Pig & Swine is not liable for the ski catastrophe. I have written that sentence down before it changes shape.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: The firm is alive, but invoiced. That still goes in the good column today.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: International Hambitrator. I do not know whether the badge helps, but it sounds expensive enough to scare the invoice.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: So he was quietly funding cases while making himself look like a financial weather event. That is... very Mr. Swine.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: A new notice arrived. It is citywide, which is rarely comforting.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Mr. Swine is back, the firm is alive, and the city appears to be preparing something worse.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: I have created a Swine-specific inbox. It is just a normal inbox with more emotional distance.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: The closure notice is on Mr. Murrow's desk. Mr. Pig is trying to interpret it optimistically. Please stop him.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: The same kind of notice went to firms, residents, and legal-aid offices. Efficiency is a terrible word when it starts flattening people.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: I have opened the final file. It immediately required a second file. That feels accurate.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Go to Protest Square in the Civic Core. The people affected by the notices are gathering there.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: You need impact statements. The Court will need to know what the notices do to actual people.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: The impact statements are thick. Consequences usually are, no matter how thin the notice paper is.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Waldek understands what a generic notice can do to a specific home. I wish he did not have to be useful that way.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: City Hall should have the official plan. I know, I also dislike that sentence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Find the notice templates. If everyone received the same notice, individual assessment may have been replaced by copy-paste.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: The plan says access to justice hub while removing everyone currently providing access to justice. Bold, in the way sinkholes are bold.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Identical notices for different lives. I have labelled the batch administratively efficient and morally suspicious.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Crab thinks the money trail matters. Crab is usually right when he looks that annoyed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: The City Official says everyone was individually assessed. The notice batch may disagree.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Apparently every individual assessment used the same words in the same order. Remarkable administrative telepathy.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: The objection system points to the Industrial Printer Facility in the Business District. Bring Bajtek and emotional distance.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: The industrial printer is still running. This is not a metaphor anymore, which is inconvenient.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Find the objection logs. If objections disappeared before review, the scheme is not just cruel. It is procedural machinery with bad manners.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Objections marked duplicate before anyone read them. I would call that efficient if I had no soul or diary.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: One print command, hundreds of legal consequences. I am beginning to dislike buttons.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: The printer room is active. Bajtek can extract logs if you keep the paper storm busy. I hate that this is a legal instruction.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Clean shutdown, recovered objections, minimal paper injuries. That may be the most elegant thing this office has ever done.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: The printer stopped, the logs survived, and no one was fully laminated. Standard victory.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Bajtek got enough logs before the machine gave up dramatically. The proof is weaker, but still breathing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: The recovered files connect to Mr. Swine's ledger. He has the face of a man about to become less simple.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: He labelled pro-bono transfers as ski expenses. I am angry, touched, and professionally confused.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: So the ridiculous firm was being secretly held together by ridiculous funding for very real people. That is a sentence only this office could earn.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Mr. Murrow opened the Team Assembly board. Assign the right people to the right legal points. Please do not assign Mr. Pig to procurement.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: One legal point still has no person attached. Courts respect structure. They also notice empty chairs.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: That assignment is brave. Brave is not always the neighbour of useful.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: The brief is viable. It has law, facts, witnesses, and only a controlled amount of Whimsy.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: That board looks like a real final brief. I am putting this moment in the calendar under improbable dignity.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: The brief is ready, but I can index it before you go. Courts respect tabs more than they admit.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: The Supreme Court is open. Bring the Final Brief, the ledger, the objection logs, and everyone who still believes this office should exist.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
context: player tries to go to Supreme Court underprepared
text: The stairs may radiate endgame energy, but the brief still needs evidence. Please do not climb into formal disaster.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: For defective notice, use the templates and someone who can explain what ordinary recipients actually received.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: For individual impact, bring people, not abstractions. Public interest should have to look Waldek in the eye.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: For technical suppression, let Bajtek speak before anyone turns automation into poetry.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: For conflict of interest, follow the procurement link. Crab already has that face.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: For social utility, the ledger matters because it shows this office did more than survive. It let people through the door.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: The judgment is in. Pig & Swine is still open. I have put that in writing before the universe changes its mind.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: The scheme is suspended, the objections return, and the office remains shabby but operational. That may be our house style.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Temporary injunction. Further review. Thin, breathing, and enough to keep the door open.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Pig & Swine is still open. I have put that in writing before anyone invents a counter-notice.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: There is already someone waiting at reception. I asked if it was urgent. They said only if rights count.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: The printer says welcome back, counsel. Then low paper. I choose to hear affection.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: I am still underpaid, still overbooked, and somehow much more difficult to frighten with official envelopes.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Check the case board. If the board looks confused, ask Mr. Murrow. If Mr. Murrow looks confused, evacuate politely.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: If anyone asks, the office is running smoothly. If no one asks, please bring me the blue folder.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: I have sorted the urgent pile into urgent, very urgent, and urgent but emotionally avoidable.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: The phone has rung seven times, the calendar is crying, and my salary remains theoretical. Anyway, how can I help?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: You are doing better than you think. The office has a way of making competence look like panic wearing shoes.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Crab acts like he does not care. Whimsy acts like he cares about everything symbolically. Mr. Murrow cares quietly. Please do not make me organise their feelings.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Start with the map, then the case board, then whoever looks least likely to set something on fire. Today, that may be you.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: We now have badges, case files, printer trauma, and a social-utility argument. I miss when the problem was only insolvency.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
text: Clients usually arrive after the system has already made them feel foolish. Please do not help the system with that.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
context: Beat 1 welcome as Cula enters on his first morning
text: Dr. A. Cula, good morning. Welcome to Pig & Swine.
notes: Phase 7 V1.1 draft; cheerful while drowning; not final committed game dialogue.

speaker: Asia
context: Beat 1 welcome; Asia hands off Cula's folder while juggling reception workload
text: The blue folder is yours. I am keeping it above the urgent pile and below the ringing phone.
notes: Phase 7 V1.1 draft; cheerful while drowning; not final committed game dialogue.

speaker: Asia
context: Beat 1 welcome; practical orientation toward Pig and Mr. Murrow
text: Mr. Pig is by the case board. Mr. Murrow is in the file area with the case folder.
notes: Phase 7 V1.1 draft; no sharp aside; not final committed game dialogue.

speaker: Asia
context: Beat 1 V1.1 pass 2 — first welcome to Cula on his first morning
text: Dr. A. Cula, good morning. We've been expecting you.
notes: Phase 7 V1.1 pass 2 draft; cheerful while drowning; not final committed game text.

speaker: Asia
context: Beat 1 V1.1 pass 2 — practical orientation: blue folder, archive, Murrow
text: Your blue folder is on the second pile from the left. The archive is through that door, and Mr. Murrow is in there with the case file whenever you're ready for him.
notes: Phase 7 V1.1 pass 2 draft; warm-functional pointer; not final.

speaker: Asia
context: Beat 1 V1.1 pass 2 — directs Cula to Mr. Pig at case board
text: Mr. Pig is at the case board. He'll want to say hello first.
notes: Phase 7 V1.1 pass 2 draft; flagged for §J read-aloud (alternative: 'Start there.'); not final.

speaker: Asia
context: Beat 8 V1.3 pass 3 — closing exchange after Cula asks if Mrs. Borowski has been called
text: She rang an hour ago, Dr. Cula. She'll be there at quarter to.
notes: Phase 7 V1.3 pass 3 draft; pass-3 addition gives Asia register at scene close and humanises the client; not final.

speaker: Asia
context: Beat 8 announce; warm-direct register; from threshold of meeting-room corridor (does not enter the meeting room)
text: Mrs. Sikorska is here. I've shown her into the meeting room.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Asia
context: Beat 13 V1.7 pass 5 — warm-while-sorting congratulation; hands continuing through folder spines; period-split per pass-4 arbitration
text: Good work, Dr. Cula. The blue file is back on your desk.
notes: Phase 7 V1.7 pass 5 commit shape; warm-while-sorting; two short declaratives per §B.2 period-split arbitration; not final.

speaker: Asia
context: Beat 14 V1.7 pass 5 — postcard address-label announcement; three short declaratives; sorting does not pause
text: Mr. Pig. Postcard. The address says Sapporo.
notes: Phase 7 V1.7 pass 5 commit shape; Beat 14 address-label; not final.

speaker: Asia
context: V1.A pass 3 — Beat 2 entry hint state; player has not yet seen Pig's Beat-2 panic line; hints toward Mr. Pig
text: You should talk to Mr. Pig first. He's the loudest crisis in the room.
notes: Phase 7 V1.A pass 3 commit shape; hint-NPC repeat line; verbatim from `_legacy/dialogue_samples.txt` line 50; not final.

speaker: Asia
context: V1.A pass 3 — Pig crisis revealed; Murrow not yet met; hints toward Mr. Murrow in the file area
text: Mr. Murrow will know what the case is actually about. He's somewhere between the files and disappointment.
notes: Phase 7 V1.A pass 3 commit shape; hint-NPC repeat line; verbatim from `_legacy/dialogue_samples.txt` line 51; not final.

speaker: Asia
context: V1.A pass 3 — Murrow met; binder not yet collected; hints toward the archive and the procedural binder
text: You're looking for the procedural binder. I tried lifting it once and briefly saw another dimension.
notes: Phase 7 V1.A pass 3 commit shape; hint-NPC repeat line; verbatim from `_legacy/dialogue_samples.txt` line 52; not final.

speaker: Asia
context: V1.A pass 3 — Binder collected; Crab not yet recruited; hints toward Crab and the service-problem angle
text: If it's a service problem, Crab will have an opinion. He always has an opinion.
notes: Phase 7 V1.A pass 3 commit shape; hint-NPC repeat line; verbatim from `_legacy/dialogue_samples.txt` line 53; not final.

speaker: Asia
context: V1.A pass 3 — Crab recruited; Rights Memo not yet collected; hints toward Whimsy at Café Paragraf
text: The rights memo may be near Whimsy. Try Café Paragraf — or wherever someone is talking too loudly about fairness.
notes: Phase 7 V1.A pass 3 commit shape; hint-NPC repeat line; verbatim from `_legacy/dialogue_samples.txt` line 54; not final.

speaker: Asia
context: V1.A pass 3 — Rights Memo collected; Whimsy not yet recruited; hints toward Whimsy and the fair-hearing angle
text: Whimsy is useful if pointed at the right legal issue. Try asking him about fair hearing.
notes: Phase 7 V1.A pass 3 commit shape; hint-NPC repeat line; verbatim from `_legacy/dialogue_samples.txt` line 55; not final.

speaker: Asia
context: V1.A pass 3 — Whimsy recruited; Halina has arrived for Beat 8 client meeting; hints toward Mrs. Sikorska in the meeting room
text: Mrs. Sikorska is in the meeting room. She brought her own folder.
notes: Phase 7 V1.A pass 3 commit shape; hint-NPC repeat line; phase-8 new line authored from §B.1 recommended sample shape; not final.

speaker: Asia
context: V1.A pass 3 — Beat 8 client meeting concluded; Murrow in archive with binder for Beat 9 research; hints toward Mr. Murrow in the archive
text: Mr. Murrow is in the archive with the binder. He's been making the disappointed-page-turn sound.
notes: Phase 7 V1.A pass 3 commit shape; hint-NPC repeat line; phase-8 new line authored from §B.1 recommended sample shape; not final.

speaker: Asia
context: V1.A pass 3 — Archive research complete; Beat 10 readiness check not yet completed; hints toward Mr. Murrow's check
text: Mr. Murrow should check the case before court. He has a special face for missing documents.
notes: Phase 7 V1.A pass 3 commit shape; hint-NPC repeat line; verbatim from `_legacy/dialogue_samples.txt` line 56; not final.

speaker: Asia
context: V1.A pass 3 — Readiness check passed; court not yet walked to; hints toward the District Court walk north
text: If Mr. Murrow says you're ready, go north to the District Court. And maybe drink water.
notes: Phase 7 V1.A pass 3 commit shape; hint-NPC repeat line; verbatim from `_legacy/dialogue_samples.txt` line 57; not final.

speaker: Asia
context: V1.A pass 3 — Court won; Beat-14 Swine postcard not yet arrived; anticipates postcard arrival
text: Something arrived from Mr. Swine. The stamp looks expensive and irresponsible.
notes: Phase 7 V1.A pass 3 commit shape; hint-NPC repeat line; verbatim from `_legacy/dialogue_samples.txt` line 58; not final.

speaker: Asia
context: V1.A pass 3 — All chapter-1 flags satisfied; post-postcard chapter coda; default state
text: For the first time today, nothing is actively on fire. I give it twelve minutes.
notes: Phase 7 V1.A pass 3 commit shape; hint-NPC repeat line; verbatim from `_legacy/dialogue_samples.txt` line 59; not final.
